codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original migration commit `c42f32a2` and the fix commit `e9a8a403`. The fix closes the two prior blocking issues: canonical migration now preserves arbitrary favorite metadata during normalization/merge, and empty prematurely-finalized canonical documents can still merge late-visible legacy favorites until a migration with real favorites has completed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The canonical watchlist migration now relies on persisted control fields `legacy_migration_checked_at`, `legacy_migrated_at`, and `legacy_migration_completed_with_favorites` to decide when late-visible legacy data should still merge versus stay suppressed after a real merge/delete cycle, but the mission shared-state docs do not record these invariants.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: app/services/favorites_service.py:243-299 implements the retry/finalization protocol; .factory/library/architecture.md documents canonical watchlist invariants but not these migration-state fields or their semantics.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed report, the fix feature transcript skeleton, both commit diffs (`c42f32a2` and `e9a8a403`), the backend-worker skill, and the shared-state docs. The fix adequately addresses the original failures by preserving arbitrary metadata fields through canonical normalization/merge and by allowing late-visible legacy favorites to merge after an empty canonical document was prematurely finalized, with focused regression tests covering both scenarios.
